title: Micropera sterrophylla
intro: Micropera sterrophylla, also known as the forest orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Micropera sterrophylla is prepared as a tea or decoction, or used in combination with other herbs in formulations such as infusions and tonics, to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Micropera sterrophylla is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the African violet family. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants such as mint and snapdragons. The order Lamiales is within the clade Asterids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families of plants like heath and bellflowers. The clade Asterids is a part of the larger clade Angiosperm, which includes all flowering plants. The classification of Micropera sterrophylla places it within the broader group of flowering plants.
